decisions.reasoning: As the first player, I should place a settlement to maximize my resource collection. The BRICK on 11 (2 pips) and WOOD on 5 (4 pips) are the most valuable resources. Settlement 0 also has a WHEAT on 4 (3 pips), which is also valuable. This placement gives access to 3 different resources, which is optimal for the first move.

As the first player, I should secure my position by building roads to connect my settlement. The road from node 0 to node 1 or node 5 is a better choice because it allows me to potentially build a settlement on node 1 or 5 in the next turn, while the road to node 20 is farther and less immediately useful.

The game just started, and the opponent already has 1 VP. I need to secure my first settlement quickly to gain 1 VP. Settlements 2, 3, and 4 are close to the robber and produce resources, but Settlement 4 is the best as it produces both brick and wood, which are essential for building. It also has a 2:1 brick port, which is valuable.

The game just started, and I need to expand my network. The road options are between nodes 4 and 15, 15 and 17, and 14 and 15. Building between nodes 4 and 15 is the best option as it connects to my starting settlement and helps form a longer road.

Blue needs to secure a settlement quickly to start producing resources. Settlement 28 is ideal as it produces wheat (3 pips) and has a 3:1 port, allowing efficient resource trading. It also provides a good starting position for further expansion.

The game just started, and I need to secure a road connection for my settlements. Building a road from node 28 to 29 or 27 to 28 are both valid options. Since both options are equally good, I will choose the first one for simplicity.

The robber is on a brick-producing tile (3, 6, 11), so rolling a 3, 6, or 11 will prevent production. Building a settlement on node 26 will block the robber from this tile, securing the brick production. No other immediate benefits are as critical.

The robber is on a brick-producing tile, so rolling a 3 or 6 would produce brick for the opponent. Building a road to connect to my settlement at node 36 will help me produce resources and also help me meet the distance rule for future settlements. The 2:1 sheep port at node 35 is also a good option, but connecting a road is more immediate and beneficial.

The best option is to move the robber to a non-producing tile, such as (0, -1, 1), which is currently producing nothing.

I have 3 wheat in hand and can trade 3 wheat for 1 sheep or wood, which would be useful for future development. Trading 3 wheat for sheep or wood will also prevent the opponent from gaining an advantage.
End the turn as we have no valuable moves. Our hand only has 1 wheat, which is not useful for building or buying cards.
